{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2007 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2007poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 60th Cannes Film Festival.{{cite news|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2007/posters/off.html|title=Posters 2007|work=festival-cannes.fr|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214061537/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2007/posters/off.html|archive-date=14 December 2013|df=dmy}} | host = [[Diane Kruger]] | number = 22 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[My Blueberry Nights]]'' | closing = ''[[Days of Darkness (2007 Canadian film)|Days of Darkness]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days]]'' | date = {{Start date|2007|05|16|df=y}} – {{End date|2007|05|27|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2006 Cannes Film Festival|2006]] | next = [[2008 Cannes Film Festival|2008]] }} The '''60th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' was held from 16 to 27 May 2007. British filmmaker [[Stephen Frears]] served as jury president for the main competition.{{cite news|url=https://www.amc.com/talk/2007/05/cannes-2007-gea-1|title=Cannes 2007 Gears Up For Premiere of New Wong Kar-Wai Film|work=AMC|access-date=6 June 2020|archive-date=3 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200903220350/https://www.amc.com/talk/2007/05/cannes-2007-gea-1|url-status=dead}} Romanian filmmaker [[Cristian Mungiu]] won the [[Palme d'Or]] for the drama film ''[[4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2007.htm|title=60ème Festival International du Film – Cannes|work=cinema-francais.fr|language=fr|access-date=28 June 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/cine/index.php?a=07|title=Cannes 2007 / Palmarés|work=cannes-fest.com|language=fr|access-date=28 June 2017}} The official poster featured [[Pedro Almodóvar]], [[Juliette Binoche]], [[Jane Campion]], [[Souleymane Cissé (film director)|Souleymane Cissé]], [[Penélope Cruz]], [[Gérard Depardieu]], [[Samuel L. Jackson]], [[Bruce Willis]] and [[Wong Kar-wai]], all photographed by [[Alex Majoli]], as a commemoration of the 60th festival edition. The festival opened with ''[[My Blueberry Nights]]'' by [[Wong Kar-wai]],{{cite news |last=Scott |first=A. O. |date=18 May 2007 |title=At Cannes, Blueberry Nights and Romanian Days |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2007/05/18/movies/18cann.html |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=The New York Times}} and closed with ''[[Days of Darkness (2007 Canadian film)|Days of Darkness]]'' by [[Denys Arcand]].{{cite news |title=Quebec filmmaker Arcand closes Cannes on comedic note |url=http://www.cbc.ca/news/arts/quebec-filmmaker-arcand-closes-cannes-on-comedic-note-1.672613 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131215113841/http://www.cbc.ca/news/arts/quebec-filmmaker-arcand-closes-cannes-on-comedic-note-1.672613 |archive-date=15 December 2013 |work=CBC News |df=dmy}} German actress [[Diane Kruger]] was the mistress of ceremonies.{{cite news |title=Diane Kruger, Master of Ceremonies for the 60th Festival de Cannes |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/49781.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224110203/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/49781.html |archive-date=24 December 2013 |access-date=14 December 2013 |work=festival-cannes.fr |df=dmy}} [[File:Un certain regard 07.jpg|thumb|200px|2007 [[Un Certain Regard]] poster by Italian comics artist [[Enrico Marini]].{{cite news|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2007/posters/ucr.html |title=Posters 2007 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214061616/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2007/posters/ucr.html |archive-date=14 December 2013 |df=dmy}}]] ==Juries== ===Main Competition=== *[[Stephen Frears]], British director - France) *''[[Yumurta]]'' by [[Semih Kaplanoglu]] (Turkey, Greece) *''[[Zoo (2007 film)|Zoo]]'' by [[Robinson Devor]] (United States) {{div col end}} ==''Tous Les Cinemas du Monde''== ''Tous Les Cinemas du Monde'' (World Cinema) began in 2005 to showcase films from a variety of different countries. From 19 May to 25 May 2007, films were screened from India, [[Lebanon]], Poland, [[Kenya]], [[Guinea]], [[Angola]], [[Slovenia]], and [[Colombia]].{{cite web|url=http://www.monstersandcritics.com/movies/features/article_1304644.php |title=Preview: Acceptance in Cannes bestows prestige and honour |work=monstersandcritics.com |date=15 May 2007 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120930195151/http://www.monstersandcritics.com/movies/features/article_1304644.php/Preview_Acceptance_in_Cannes_bestows_prestige_and_honour |archive-date=30 September 2012 |df=dmy}}[https://www.variety.com/index.asp?layout=cannes2007&jump=features&id=2542&articleid=VR1117965596 Cannes, India celebrate 60 years (needs subscription)] ===India=== The first two days of this program held during 19 May to 25 May 2007 featured special screening of Indian films; ''[[Saira (film)|Saira]]'' (2005), ''[[Missed Call (film)|Missed Call]]'' (2005), ''[[Lage Raho Munna Bhai]]'' (2006), ''[[Dosar]]'' (2006), ''[[Veyil (2006 film)|Veyil]],'' (2006), ''[[Guru (2007 film)|Guru]]'' (2007), ''[[Dhan Dhana Dhan Goal]]'' (2007), and ''[[Dharm (film)|Dharm]]'' (2007).{{cite news |url=http://entertainment.timesonline.co.uk/tol/arts_and_entertainment/film/cannes/article1830602.ece |first=Anil |last=Sinanan |title=The Sun Rises on the East |work=[[The Times]] |publisher=The Times |date=24 May 2007 |location=London |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080708215833/http://entertainment.timesonline.co.uk/tol/arts_and_entertainment/film/cannes/article1830602.ece |archive-date=8 July 2008 |df=dmy}} ===Lebanon=== Debuting at the Director's Fortnight was [[Nadine Labaki]]'s [[Caramel (film)|Caramel]], a charming dramedy about five women who gather at a beauty salon and deal with their everyday problems with men, social expectation, sexuality, and tradition vs. modernizing times. Labaki not only directed and co-wrote the film but plays the lead as well. The rest of the cast is composed mostly of unprofessional actors, all of whom deliver very convincing performances and add a lot of color and depth to the film.{{cite news|first=A. O. |last=Scott |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2008/02/01/movies/01cara.html |title=A Haircut, With an Affair and Highlights of Support |publisher=nytimes |date=1 February 2008 |access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite news |url=http://www.spiritualityandpractice.com/films/films.php?id=17810 |first=Mary Ann |last=Brussat |title=Film Review |publisher=spiritualityandpractice |access-date=26 May 2017}} Reminiscent of a [[Pedro Almodóvar]] picture, [[Caramel (film)|Caramel]] is unique not just for its technical and creative sophistication but also for not tackling any of the religious, political, or war-related issues that have continued to plague its setting, [[Lebanon]], til now. The film proved to be a sleeper at the festival and was distributed in well over 40 countries, becoming an international hit.{{cite news|url=http://www.viewlondon.co.uk/cinemas/nadine-labaki-interview-feature-2468.html |title=Nadine Labaki Interview |publisher=viewlondon.co.uk |access-date=26 May 2017}}[[File:Stephen Frears Filmfest 2010.jpg|thumb|190px|[[Naomi Kawase]], Gran Prix winner]] [[Image:U2 Cannes 2007.jpg|thumb|U2 perform at the 2007 Cannes Film Festival, prior to the screening of ''[[U2 3D]]'']] ===In
